description: Ordered, reversible Chromium integration patch series for Project Seoul. Applied over the pinned baseRevision after Seoul-owned source is materialized. Patch 1 wires the organization service; patch 2 wires the inbound lifecycle bridge target and depends on patch 1.

entrySchema.rationale: why it is necessary and which upstream alternative was rejected
entrySchema.baseRevision: full Chromium SHA this patch is authored against; must equal manifest baseRevision
entrySchema.order: unique ascending integer giving application order
entrySchema.file: patch file path relative to patchDir
affectedPaths: upstream src-relative paths the patch touches
entrySchema.sha256: lowercase hex sha256 of the patch file
entrySchema.applyVerify: command/strategy proving the patch applies cleanly (git apply --check)
entrySchema.reverseVerify: command/strategy proving the patch reverses cleanly (git apply -R --check)
entrySchema.reviewable: true once the patch is independently reviewable in isolation

patches.description: Add the Seoul organization service to the //chrome/browser GN graph and register its profile-keyed factory at profile-service init.
patches.rationale: The profile-scoped SeoulOrganizationService must be built so its bounded preference is registered and the default workspace is initialized for eligible regular profiles. Adding a GN dependency edge plus one factory GetInstance() registration is the smallest upstream change; alternatives (a global static initializer, or editing browser_prefs.cc directly) were rejected as less localized and harder to reverse. Exposes no UI and changes no tab behavior.

patches.description: Add the Seoul inbound lifecycle bridge target (//seoul/browser/lifecycle) to the //chrome/browser GN graph so the per-profile window watcher, per-window tab-strip bridges, coordinator, and persistence scheduler are built and linked alongside the already-registered organization service. No new registration site is needed: the service (patch 1) constructs and owns the bridge.
patches.rationale: The lifecycle code is repository-owned Seoul source that observes Chromium window/tab/split events and feeds the organization model. It is pulled transitively through the organization target, but is declared as an explicit, independently reviewable GN dependency edge so the bridge is a first-class build input and can be reviewed and reverted on its own. No UI, command, AI, or session-storage change is introduced. Depends on patch 1 (the organization dep line is its context anchor and the service owns the bridge).
patches.applyVerify: git -C $SEOUL_CHROMIUM_ROOT/src apply (after patch 1); cumulative series round trip in native/scripts/patches.sh verify
patches.reverseVerify: git -C $SEOUL_CHROMIUM_ROOT/src apply -R 0002 then 0001 (descending); checkout restored byte-identical
